Use @RequiredArgsConstructor here to be consistent with others places.

We can refactor as below : (Avoid String.format if we can)

 entityManager.createNativeQuery(
                            DROP_FRAGMENT_CONSTRAINT
                                    + ADD_FRAGMENT_CONSTRAINT_WITH_CASCADE
                                    + "DELETE FROM fragment f USING "+tempTableName+" t WHERE f.anchor_id = :anchorId AND f.xpath = t.xpath;"
                                    + DROP_FRAGMENT_CONSTRAINT
                                    + ADD_ORIGINAL_FRAGMENT_CONSTRAINT)
            .setParameter("anchorId", anchorId)
            .executeUpdate();

Same as above :

 entityManager.createNativeQuery(DROP_FRAGMENT_CONSTRAINT
                            + ADD_FRAGMENT_CONSTRAINT_WITH_CASCADE
                            + "DELETE FROM fragment f USING " + tempTableName
                            + " t WHERE f.anchor_id = :anchorId AND f.xpath LIKE CONCAT(t.xpath, :xpathPattern);"
                            + DROP_FRAGMENT_CONSTRAINT
                            + ADD_ORIGINAL_FRAGMENT_CONSTRAINT)
                    .setParameter("anchorId", anchorId)
                    .setParameter("xpathPattern", "[%%")
                    .executeUpdate();
Rename as createTempXpathTable or more suitable varaible name.

I tried above changes and variations, but Sonar check fails because of potential SQL injection (java:S2077). Strangely, it does not fail with the current code. I can make the changes you propose and suppress warning for java:S2077. What do you think? (Regarding warning, there's no SQL vulnerability here since the table name is generated internally without user input.)
I think could be worth making a new method in TempTableCreator that just takes a list of Strings, looked at original use of method and the Lists have more than one entry where this only has one

I always thought it was a flaw that the client need to know whether the thing being deleted was a list or not. I see now you method handles both anyway and wonder if we cab use call is delete DataNodes and it ending up an overlaoded method of the method on line 601

really would like if we can start using Luke's new integration test for this. The point was to avoid adding more tests like these (you can opt to do this in a separate commit but I think we should do it before closing teh story)
can we make the number bigger now (eg 50) to more accurately show the speed
I doing fully understand the difference with the previous test
I see you changed teh name but not teh description. Not 100% clear why you changed teh name might be good to have a little chat
not suer if you need to be so strict I probably would have tested with 2 * and use _ for the path parameter
same comment as on patch14, just adding here for visibility
agreed to rename to deleteDataNodes and make it delete anything
once we agree to have one common method that can delete anything this parameter name will become generic and decision no longer needed
NB This metric has been renamed to "cps.data.service.datanode.all.delete", and the new batch delete is called "cps.data.service.datanode.batch.delete"
Edited commit message with this info
This batch size and timing can be increased to get more meaningful result
This batch size and timing can be increased to get more meaningful result
Should be called 'Batch delete one large node'

NB: I think we can deprecate this method soon
(maybe apply that in the next related commit)
can/should this just redirect to deleteDataNode method now?!

Depends if you care about exceptions:
- deleteDataNode() throws DataNodeNotFound for non-existing xpath (can be datanode, list, list element)
- deleteDataNodes() does not throw exception for non-existing
- deleteListOrListElement() throws DataNodeNotFound if the list or list element doesn't exist - even if a non-list datanode with same xpath does exist

Unless there's a need to specifically delete only list nodes, then yes we can redirect it.
